skill.name: subagent-development
skill.description: Guides iterative implementation by assigning each plan task to a fresh subagent and requiring review checkpoints between tasks.
skill.summary: Run isolated subagent implementation cycles with code review gates after each task.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak crypto, and network reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, placeholder task text, and ordinary workflow language, with no executable code or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The detected backticks are Markdown code fences around prompt templates and review templates. No executable Ruby, shell command, or command substitution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is a Markdown fence or template boundary in documentation. There is no executable file, interpreter directive, or user input passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the frontmatter description and placeholder task workflow text. No hash function, cipher, password handling, or cryptographic operation appears in these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contents are descriptive prose and placeholders. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic algorithms was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss test verification and architecture review. No host discovery, port scanning, network command, URL, or remote endpoint is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching text is normal software delivery language about tests and architecture. I did not find evidence of scanning tools, network probing, or data collection.

content.value_statement: Complex implementation plans can lose quality when one context handles every task. This skill structures work into isolated subagent tasks with review gates after each step.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use isolated subagents for implementation work.
Provides a step-by-step process for loading a plan and creating tasks.
Supplies prompt templates for implementation and code review subagents.
Defines severity-based actions for review feedback.
Provides a final review checkpoint before merge or pull request work.
Includes a reusable quality gate template for task reviews.
limitations: It does not include executable automation or scripts.
It assumes compatible subagent and reviewer tools are available.
It works best when plan tasks are mostly independent.
It does not replace project-specific tests or human review judgment.

use_cases.title: Execute a planned feature safely
use_cases.description: Break a feature plan into isolated task cycles with review after each completed unit.

use_cases.title: Maintain review discipline across AI work
use_cases.description: Require review checkpoints before the assistant moves from one implementation task to the next.

use_cases.title: Coordinate long AI coding sessions
use_cases.description: Use fresh contexts to reduce carryover errors during multi-step implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Task Cycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the subagent-development workflow for this plan. Create the task list, run the first task with a fresh subagent, then review it before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Completed Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the review checkpoint for the completed task. Compare the implementation with requirements, assess tests, and classify findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Independent Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this implementation plan with one fresh subagent per independent task. Stop after any critical or high issue until it is resolved.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Full Iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Manage this multi-task implementation end to end. Use isolated subagents, commit-level review context, deferred issue tracking, and a final readiness review.

output_examples.input: Run the workflow for a three-task feature plan.
output: A task list is created from the plan.
The first task is assigned to a fresh implementation subagent.
A code review checkpoint reports status, severity, and next action.

output_examples.input: Review the implementation of task two before continuing.
output: The review compares the implementation with the task requirements.
Findings are grouped as critical, high, medium, or minor.
The workflow either proceeds, fixes issues, or requests discussion.

output_examples.input: Finish the full plan and prepare for merge.
output: A final review checks plan compliance, architecture, regressions, and readiness.
Deferred issues are documented for follow-up.
The workflow transitions to merge or pull request work.

best_practices: Use this workflow only after the implementation plan is clear and reviewed.
Keep each subagent task small enough for focused implementation and review.
Resolve critical and high review findings before assigning the next task.
anti_patterns: Do not run several implementation subagents in parallel for dependent tasks.
Do not skip review checkpoints to save time.
Do not use this workflow for small changes that do not need context isolation.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant execute a plan through isolated implementation tasks and repeated code review checkpoints.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown workflow guide and does not include executable scripts or automation.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the plan is unclear, tasks are tightly dependent, or the change is simple.

faq.question: How does it handle review feedback?
faq.answer: It classifies feedback by severity and requires critical or high issues to be fixed before continuing.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for human code review?
faq.answer: No. It improves review discipline but still depends on project tests, maintainers, and human judgment.
